jokes.text: Comma, bald category.
jokes.setup: Kenneth calls Jack his best friend
jokes.punchline: The bizarre categorical qualifier delivered like email syntax
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character voice - his childlike literal thinking expressed through absurd categorization system

jokes.text: Comma, beautiful hair category, parentheses, strong.
jokes.setup: Kenneth uses the same categorization system again
jokes.punchline: Escalates the absurdity with even more specific qualifiers
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that builds on the established pattern while adding new absurd detail

jokes.text: They were orange and had hearts for pockets.
jokes.setup: Jack reveals childhood poverty about wearing sister's hand-me-downs
jokes.punchline: The specific humiliating detail of hearts for pockets
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that humanizes Jack while being genuinely funny and pathetic

jokes.text: I'm a mom. Give me a break.
jokes.setup: Nancy gives a convoluted description of a movie/actors
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of her mom-brain confusion
jokes.explanation: Relatable character moment with good self-awareness

jokes.text: I was, but I forgot my bag of hair.
jokes.setup: Paul asks why Jenna didn't get her hair done
jokes.punchline: The casually mentioned 'bag of hair' as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Jenna line - the casual mention of something completely bizarre

jokes.text: It's not just the name of a bank that sued me.
jokes.setup: Jenna talks about fidelity in relationships
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was sued by Fidelity bank as an aside
jokes.explanation: Great throwaway line that reveals Jenna's chaotic financial life

jokes.text: God, he came out of nowhere.
jokes.setup: Kenneth appears suddenly
jokes.punchline: Jack's startled reaction to Kenneth's ninja-like appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy beat but well-executed for Kenneth's character

jokes.text: Tickle each other like teletubbies.
jokes.setup: Nancy explains what she told her kids about adult activities
jokes.punchline: The bizarre comparison to teletubbies
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird euphemism that's both innocent and disturbing

jokes.text: Two spider-mans fighting, And sometimes they make weird noises, But they're not hurting each other.
jokes.setup: Nancy continues her bizarre explanations to kids
jokes.punchline: Even more absurd metaphor involving Spider-Man
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the euphemism pattern with more specific absurdity

jokes.text: How often did they walk in on you? A lot.
jokes.setup: Jack asks about the frequency of interruptions
jokes.punchline: Nancy's matter-of-fact 'A lot' admission
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan delivery that adds to the cringe

jokes.text: D'oh, bother! Treble bother!
jokes.setup: Wesley expresses frustration
jokes.punchline: His pretentious, affected way of swearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice for pretentious Wesley - archaic, affected language

jokes.text: I vowed on the snipes name I would never set foot in there again.
jokes.setup: Wesley explains why he won't go get his shoes
jokes.punchline: The grandiose 'vow on the Snipes name' for something trivial
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Wesley's melodramatic approach to minor slights

jokes.text: Specializing in the transportation industry. Fulfills my boyhood passion for train accidents.
jokes.setup: Wesley explains his insurance work
jokes.punchline: His disturbing childhood fascination with train accidents
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals Wesley's twisted psychology while being genuinely funny

jokes.text: That's how I got out of doing foreplay with Angie.
jokes.setup: Tracy advises Kenneth to do a bad job to avoid promotion
jokes.punchline: TMI revelation about avoiding foreplay the same way
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy overshare that's both inappropriate and perfectly in character

jokes.text: Get it yourself, Chubbs. I'm on a coffee break.
jokes.setup: Kenneth is supposed to be doing a bad job
jokes.punchline: His hilariously mild version of being rude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - his idea of being bad is adorably mild

jokes.text: That's my boy.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's mild rudeness
jokes.punchline: Tracy's proud reaction to Kenneth's pathetic attempt at badness
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Tracy's low standards for rebellion

jokes.text: Like a s'more you could take a shower with?
jokes.setup: Jack talks about combining two women into one perfect woman
jokes.punchline: The bizarre s'more shower metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd metaphor that's both sexual and completely nonsensical

jokes.text: Like at a haunted house sex party. Exactly.
jokes.setup: Jack talks about wondering about other choices
jokes.punchline: The random haunted house sex party metaphor that Paul accepts
jokes.explanation: Another wonderfully random Jack metaphor made funnier by Paul's acceptance

jokes.text: Does the 'o' have it? 'o,' we do. Do you 'o'? 'o,' no? Let go. 'o.'
jokes.setup: Avery recalls her embarrassing commercial work
jokes.punchline: The awful wordplay of the overshoppe.com commercial
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad commercial copy that works as cringe humor

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm a doorman... To the sky. I'm a pilot.
jokes.setup: Carol is mistaken for a doorman
jokes.punchline: His poetic way of revealing he's a pilot
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that establishes Carol's personality and profession

jokes.text: I know. People wear flip-flops to church.
jokes.setup: Discussion about declining standards
jokes.punchline: Carol's specific complaint about flip-flops in church
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Carol's traditional values

jokes.text: Sky law. It's when I put on the 'fasten seatbelt' sign, And no one's allowed to move till we've had ten minutes of silence. I made the whole thing up.
jokes.setup: Carol explains how he handles unruly passengers
jokes.punchline: The completely made-up 'sky law' that passengers believe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept that's both absurd and believable, perfect character establishment

jokes.text: But she was bagging pretty hard on my TGS.
jokes.setup: Carol explains why he flipped off a passenger
jokes.punchline: His personal investment in defending TGS
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carol's unexpected loyalty to the show

jokes.text: 'He who spelt it, dealt it.'
jokes.setup: Carol recounts the fart doctor sketch
jokes.punchline: The childish pun in the context of a medical sketch
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that works within the context of describing the show-within-show

jokes.text: Ring-ring. Hmm. What's up? Nothing. Just giving a dumb tour to a bunch of uggos.
jokes.setup: Kenneth pretends to take a phone call
jokes.punchline: His hilariously inappropriate fake conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth attempt at being bad - inappropriate but still somehow innocent

jokes.text: Let's meet up later And smoke some drug cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Kenneth continues his fake rebellious phone call
jokes.punchline: His hilariously naive way of referencing drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth line - his innocent attempt at sounding edgy with 'drug cigarettes'

jokes.text: You know what a great pilot would have done? Not hit the birds.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Sully Sullenberger
jokes.punchline: Carol's cynical take on the heroic pilot
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and logical take that deflates hero worship with simple logic

jokes.text: The usual ladies' room nonsense. Girl with boyfriend troubles. Someone forgot tampons. Everybody's bad at science and math.
jokes.setup: Jack asks what took Nancy so long in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Nancy's casual misogynistic generalizations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nancy's blunt, un-PC attitude

jokes.text: Smug 40-year-old bridesmaid. What a treat for everyone.
jokes.setup: Nancy sees Liz at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Her cutting assessment of Liz's situation
jokes.explanation: Sharp, mean observation that establishes Nancy's no-nonsense personality

jokes.text: Like a spray tan that won't take because your skin is too oily.
jokes.setup: Liz talks about fate washing over you
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically specific and unflattering metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz metaphor - specific, gross, and relatable to her character

jokes.text: It's made by those who do do, Which is what made me the man I am. I do do.
jokes.setup: Jack talks about people who take action
jokes.punchline: His unintentional repeated use of 'do do' sounding like bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Good juvenile humor made funnier by Jack's serious delivery

jokes.text: Everyone there smiles creepily all the time. And that's sort of my thing.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining why he doesn't want to go to LA
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission about his own creepy smile
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth self-awareness about his unsettling demeanor

jokes.text: Well, I like how she's less hot than you.
jokes.setup: Nancy asks what's special about Avery
jokes.punchline: Jack's accidentally insulting honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - brutally honest in a way that's accidentally hurtful

jokes.text: Her laugh is like music. Really mean music.
jokes.setup: Jack describes what he likes about Avery
jokes.punchline: The qualifier that her laugh is 'mean music'
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that starts romantic and becomes oddly specific and concerning

jokes.text: According to her, flat shoes are for quitters.
jokes.setup: Jack explains why Avery wears high heels
jokes.punchline: Avery's extreme philosophy about footwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail that shows Avery's intense, competitive nature

jokes.text: And by the way, when I blew your mind last night, I was giving it about 50%.
jokes.setup: Nancy is leaving Jack
jokes.punchline: Her parting shot about their physical relationship
jokes.explanation: Good exit line that's both a burn and characteristically blunt for Nancy

jokes.text: Your behavior as a fiancee Has been as weak as American tea.
jokes.setup: Wesley confronts Liz about their engagement
jokes.punchline: His pretentious insult comparing her to weak tea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wesley line - pretentious, affected, and oddly specific

jokes.text: What if the bachelorette party theme was sluts?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Liz's engagement
jokes.punchline: Cerie's random inappropriate suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical Cerie inappropriateness that fits her character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Like Carroll O'Connor from Nick at nite. Exactly, Cerie. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Liz mentions her boyfriend Carol
jokes.punchline: Cerie's random TV reference that Liz gratefully accepts
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Liz's desperation for validation

jokes.text: And someday, when Carol sees my disgusting foot secret, He's gonna be okay with it.
jokes.setup: Liz talks about her future with Carol
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of her 'disgusting foot secret'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - self-aware about her grossness but optimistic about love

jokes.text: You know there isn't.
jokes.setup: Wesley claims there's only one Wesley Snipes in the world
jokes.punchline: Liz's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan correction of Wesley's grandiose claim

jokes.text: On time. I'm a pilot.
jokes.setup: Carol says he's going to depart
jokes.punchline: His professional explanation for the aviation terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carol's professional mindset even in personal situations

jokes.text: Is their gay juice in the champagne?
jokes.setup: Jack proposes and Avery reacts to the romantic atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Her concern about 'gay juice' in the champagne
jokes.explanation: Perfect Avery line - homophobic but in a hilariously absurd way

jokes.text: I wipe the floor with that bitch.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Soledad O'Brien balancing career and motherhood
jokes.punchline: Avery's competitive, vulgar dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Avery's ruthless competitiveness

jokes.text: I wipe the floor with that bitch.
jokes.setup: Comparing herself to Soledad O'Brien as working mother
jokes.punchline: Aggressively competitive assessment of fellow journalist
jokes.explanation: Good Avery competitiveness but fairly standard aggressive character territory

jokes.text: After the other location couldn't support the weight Of Grizz's extended family.
jokes.setup: Tracy explains why the reception is at 30 Rock
jokes.punchline: The implication about his family's size/weight
jokes.explanation: Mild fat joke that's more about building character background than clever humor

jokes.text: I wonder what that Somali pirate's deal is. I could live on a boat.
jokes.setup: Liz is alone and looking at other options
jokes.punchline: Her desperate consideration of the Somali pirate
jokes.explanation: Good Liz moment showing her desperation and ability to rationalize anything

jokes.text: Yeah. Let's go lez. No, I meant, like, a book club or something. Jeez.
jokes.setup: Talk about female friendship and having each other
jokes.punchline: Liz's clarification about what she meant
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with typical Liz awkwardness

jokes.text: You have a lot of taped-up balls To come here dressed like that.
jokes.setup: Paul shows up dressed as Jenna
jokes.punchline: Jenna's malapropism mixing 'balls' with 'nerve'
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna malapropism that's both crude and somehow makes sense

jokes.text: Wrong, Jack. It's a warlord's concubine dashiki.
jokes.setup: Jack identifies Liz's outfit
jokes.punchline: Liz's more dramatic and inappropriate correction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Liz's commitment to the bit and cultural insensitivity

jokes.text: You walk briskly in a pilot's uniform, You can go pretty much anywhere. I've been upstairs in the white house while the obamas were sleeping.
jokes.setup: Carol explains how he got past security
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of breaking into the White House
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that's both terrifying and hilarious, perfect Carol character moment

jokes.text: As long as you're not Jewish. I'm totally kidding.
jokes.setup: Carol and Liz decide to give their relationship a shot
jokes.punchline: His casual antisemitic comment followed by quick backtrack
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor that establishes Carol as potentially problematic

jokes.text: That's a girl's name. It's a family name.
jokes.setup: Carol introduces himself
jokes.punchline: The observation about his feminine name and his defensive response
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about gender and names

jokes.text: You won't have Kenneth Ellen Parcell to kick around anymore,
jokes.setup: Kenneth starts his farewell speech
jokes.punchline: Reference to Nixon's famous quote with his full name including feminine middle name
jokes.explanation: Good reference that fits Kenneth's character and reveals his feminine middle name

jokes.text: Your East coast media elite problems, Your apartment renovations, And your overpriced star wars memorabilia.
jokes.setup: Kenneth lists what he's had to listen to
jokes.punchline: His specific and accurate observations about their privileged complaints
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kenneth's perceptive take on their privilege

jokes.text: I have watched you Throw away better food Than my family eats at Christmas. And I have loved it.
jokes.setup: Kenneth continues his observations about their waste
jokes.punchline: The twist that despite the inequality, he's loved it
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny moment that captures Kenneth's pure character perfectly

jokes.text: So kiss my face!
jokes.setup: Kenneth's dramatic farewell
jokes.punchline: His innocent version of 'kiss my ass'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth line - his innocent attempt at being rebellious is adorable and hilarious
